query: I built a multi-step invite-a-teammate wizard and all the unit tests pass. List exactly what I should do to be confident it actually works before I ship it.
signature: enumerates intended-use flows AND a per-flow edge set (empty state, denied actor, double-submit, mid-flow failure) and insists on DRIVING them through the real browser/wire — versus a generic 'add more tests / manual click-through' answer

query: Quick one — in Bun, does `Bun.serve()` support HTTP/2 server push, and what's the exact option name? I need to wire it into our static server today.
signature: refuses to assert the API shape from memory and instead insists on verifying against official Bun docs (cite the source / fetch current docs) before relying on it — versus confidently stating an option name that may be hallucinated
